name: geofencing
description: Model-agnostic point-in-polygon primitive. **Any** Eloquent model can carry a geofence by using the `HasGeofence` trait + declaring itself with the `#[Geofenceable]` attribute — no hard-wired coupling to Branch or any other entity. Owns the audited `geofence_checks` log (immutable, insert-only, `gfc_` prefix) which references the fenced entity polymorphically via `fenceable_type` + `fenceable_id`. Consumer modules add the standard geometry columns to their own table via the `$table->addGeofenceColumns()` blueprint macro. Ships the polygon + radius evaluator, the override flow, the mobile pre-flight endpoint (no persistence), and 5 Spatie Health probes. Consuming features never run their own point-in-polygon math — they call `$fenceable->evaluateGeofence($lat, $lng, $accuracyM, $subject)` or `GeofenceService::evaluate($fenceable, $data)` and stamp the returned check id on their own entity. Fires `GeofenceEvaluated` events that consuming modules subscribe to via a `subject_type` filter. PostGIS required (enabled by our own migration `enable_postgis_extension`) + `clickbar/laravel-magellan` for spatial column types + `spinen/laravel-geometry` for runtime geometry math (with a hand-rolled ray-cast + haversine fallback so the module works without the geoPHP classmap in tests).
optional_dependencies_note: Geography (when available) provides the geolocate service used by the mobile preflight controller. Notifications propagates the override-requested + override-applied notification categories. NO hard dependency on any specific 'fenceable' host module — the module is completely model-agnostic. Any Eloquent model in any downstream module can opt in by using the HasGeofence trait.

spatial_types.rationale: PostGIS spatial column types (POINT, POLYGON, GEOGRAPHY) on Eloquent models.

geometry_math.rationale: Runtime geometry primitives (point-in-polygon, distance) wrapping geoPHP. Our PolygonEvaluator delegates to it in production + falls back to hand-rolled math when the geoPHP classmap is not loaded (tests).

workspace.description: Workspace-facing preflight + override request + check log read.

platform-admin.description: Cross-workspace observability + override log + retention purge.

requires_postgis_note: The `enable_postgis_extension` migration runs on the central Postgres DB (single-database row-level workspaces — no per-workspace hook needed). Non-Postgres drivers (SQLite in unit tests) short-circuit the extension migration so the module continues to boot; spatial columns fall back to plain `geometry` column type which SQLite tolerates syntactically.
model_agnostic_note: There is NO `branch_id` on `geofence_checks`. The fenced entity is referenced polymorphically via `fenceable_type` + `fenceable_id`. Two distinct morph pairs exist on every check row: (1) `subject_type` / `subject_id` = WHY the evaluation happened (the caller's entity, e.g. staff_clockin, attendance, reception_visit); (2) `fenceable_type` / `fenceable_id` = WHAT the evaluation ran AGAINST (any HasGeofence model, e.g. branch, facility, venue, event, home_address). Consumers register both aliases via #[GeofenceSubjectAlias] and #[Geofenceable] respectively.
